records.name: c0ba370e-7432-cfc9-9997-c67799d9a708
records.notes: The Municipal Financial Capacity Grant (MFCG) is an unconditional grant to support municipalities and towns in providing services to Nova Scotians. MFCG is legislated under the Municipal Grants Act, with the specific formula calculating the grant detailed in the accompanying regulations. The formula, modernized in 2024-25, attempts to measure a municipality’s cost to deliver a set of municipal services against its ability to pay for those services, when compared to similar municipalities. Under the modernized formula, some municipalities experienced a decrease in funding levels and a 5-year Transitional Support Program was established to ease the transition for the impacted municipalities.

notes_translated.en: The Municipal Financial Capacity Grant (MFCG) is an unconditional grant to support municipalities and towns in providing services to Nova Scotians. MFCG is legislated under the Municipal Grants Act, with the specific formula calculating the grant detailed in the accompanying regulations. The formula, modernized in 2024-25, attempts to measure a municipality’s cost to deliver a set of municipal services against its ability to pay for those services, when compared to similar municipalities. Under the modernized formula, some municipalities experienced a decrease in funding levels and a 5-year Transitional Support Program was established to ease the transition for the impacted municipalities.
notes_translated.fr-t-en: La Subvention pour la capacité financière des municipalités (MFCG) est une subvention inconditionnelle visant à aider les municipalités et les villes à fournir des services aux Néo-Écossais. La MFCG est régie par la loi sur les subventions municipales, avec une formule spécifique de calcul de la subvention détaillée dans les règlements d'accompagnement. La formule, modernisée en 2024-2025, tente de mesurer le coût de la prestation d'un ensemble de services municipaux par rapport à sa capacité de payer pour ces services, par rapport à des municipalités similaires. Dans le cadre de la formule modernisée, certaines municipalités ont connu une baisse de leurs niveaux de financement et un programme de soutien transitoire quinquennal a été mis en place pour faciliter la transition pour les municipalités touchées.

records.name: 503bc981-b23b-51f2-ee8e-cdb4f0368ba0
records.notes: One of the action items of the Sustainable Transportation Strategy, was to develop a grant program focused on providing funds to communities for sustainable transportation related projects. The grant program, Nova Scotia Moves was launched in 2012/2013 and went on for 2 fiscal years after that. It was restructured and renamed to Connect2 in 2015/2016.

notes_translated.en: One of the action items of the Sustainable Transportation Strategy, was to develop a grant program focused on providing funds to communities for sustainable transportation related projects. The grant program, Nova Scotia Moves was launched in 2012/2013 and went on for 2 fiscal years after that. It was restructured and renamed to Connect2 in 2015/2016.
notes_translated.fr-t-en: L'une des mesures prises dans le cadre de la Stratégie de transport durable consistait à élaborer un programme de subventions visant à fournir des fonds aux collectivités pour des projets liés au transport durable. Le programme de subventions, Nova Scotia Moves, a été lancé en 2012-2013 et s'est poursuivi pendant deux exercices financiers par la suite. Il a été restructuré et renommé Connect2 en 2015/2016.

records.notes: The MRDF is designed to assist prospectors, exploration companies, and researchers, employ post-secondary students, and support projects in the mining sector that attract investment and grow Nova Scotia's economy and create jobs, especially in rural areas. The data published includes the file number, the Grant recipient, the applicant, the Company, the Project Name, the Commodity and the Amount awarded.

notes_translated.en: The MRDF is designed to assist prospectors, exploration companies, and researchers, employ post-secondary students, and support projects in the mining sector that attract investment and grow Nova Scotia's economy and create jobs, especially in rural areas. The data published includes the file number, the Grant recipient, the applicant, the Company, the Project Name, the Commodity and the Amount awarded.
notes_translated.fr-t-en: Le MRDF est conçu pour aider les prospecteurs, les sociétés d'exploration et les chercheurs, à employer des étudiants de niveau postsecondaire et à soutenir des projets dans le secteur minier qui attirent des investissements, font croître l'économie de la Nouvelle-Écosse et créent des emplois, en particulier dans les zones rurales. Les données publiées incluent le numéro de dossier, le bénéficiaire de la subvention, le demandeur, la société, le nom du projet, la marchandise et le montant accordé.

records.name: ec676496-a50d-4afa-9a2f-4d97748e79e5
records.notes:
Supplementary Estimates are part of the normal parliamentary approval process to ensure that previously planned government initiatives receive the necessary funding to move them forward, therefore meeting the needs of Canadians. They present information to Parliament on the Government of Canada’s spending requirements that were not sufficiently developed in time for inclusion in the Main Estimates, or urgent but unforeseen, such as natural disasters. Supplementary Estimates directly support an appropriation act.

Supplementary Estimates are published throughout the year; each release is identified alphabetically (A, B, C, etc.). In general, Supplementary Estimates (A) are tabled in May and the associated appropriation act is granted royal assent and becomes law in June. These provide federal organizations with the funding they need early in the year. Supplementary Estimates (B) are tabled in late October or early November and the associated appropriation act is granted royal assent in December. Supplementary Estimates (C) are tabled in February and the associated appropriation act is granted royal assent in March.

notes_translated.en:
Supplementary Estimates are part of the normal parliamentary approval process to ensure that previously planned government initiatives receive the necessary funding to move them forward, therefore meeting the needs of Canadians. They present information to Parliament on the Government of Canada’s spending requirements that were not sufficiently developed in time for inclusion in the Main Estimates, or urgent but unforeseen, such as natural disasters. Supplementary Estimates directly support an appropriation act.

Supplementary Estimates are published throughout the year; each release is identified alphabetically (A, B, C, etc.). In general, Supplementary Estimates (A) are tabled in May and the associated appropriation act is granted royal assent and becomes law in June. These provide federal organizations with the funding they need early in the year. Supplementary Estimates (B) are tabled in late October or early November and the associated appropriation act is granted royal assent in December. Supplementary Estimates (C) are tabled in February and the associated appropriation act is granted royal assent in March.
notes_translated.fr:
Les budgets supplémentaires des dépenses font partie du processus normal d’approbation parlementaire qui permet de veiller à ce que les initiatives gouvernementales planifiées au préalable reçoivent le financement nécessaire pour aller de l’avant et répondre aux besoins des Canadiens. Ils présentent au Parlement de l’information sur les besoins en matière de dépenses du gouvernement du Canada qui n’étaient pas suffisamment élaborés au moment du dépôt du budget principal des dépenses, ou qui sont urgents mais imprévus, par exemple en cas de catastrophes naturelles. Les budgets supplémentaires des dépenses appuient directement une loi de crédits.

Les budgets supplémentaires des dépenses sont publiés au cours de l’exercice et désignés en ordre alphabétique (A, B, C, etc.). En général, le budget supplémentaire des dépenses (A) est déposé en mai, et la loi de crédits qui s’y rattache reçoit la sanction royale et devient loi en juin. Il fournit aux organisations fédérales les fonds dont elles ont besoin au début de l’année. Puis, le budget supplémentaire des dépenses (B) est déposé à la fin d’octobre ou au début de novembre, et la loi de crédits qui s’y rattache reçoit la sanction royale en décembre. Quant au budget supplémentaire des dépenses (C), il est déposé en février, et la loi de crédits qui s’y rattache reçoit la sanction royale en mars.

records.name: 285a0172-379a-495a-b5aa-2dccc84ed960
records.notes:
Supplementary Estimates are part of the normal parliamentary approval process to ensure that previously planned government initiatives receive the necessary funding to move them forward, therefore meeting the needs of Canadians. They present information to Parliament on the Government of Canada’s spending requirements that were not sufficiently developed in time for inclusion in the Main Estimates, or urgent but unforeseen, such as natural disasters. Supplementary Estimates directly support an appropriation act.

Supplementary Estimates are published throughout the year; each release is identified alphabetically (A, B, C, etc.). In general, Supplementary Estimates (A) are tabled in May and the associated appropriation act is granted royal assent and becomes law in June. These provide federal organizations with the funding they need early in the year. Supplementary Estimates (B) are tabled in late October or early November and the associated appropriation act is granted royal assent in December. Supplementary Estimates (C) are tabled in February and the associated appropriation act is granted royal assent in March.

notes_translated.en:
Supplementary Estimates are part of the normal parliamentary approval process to ensure that previously planned government initiatives receive the necessary funding to move them forward, therefore meeting the needs of Canadians. They present information to Parliament on the Government of Canada’s spending requirements that were not sufficiently developed in time for inclusion in the Main Estimates, or urgent but unforeseen, such as natural disasters. Supplementary Estimates directly support an appropriation act.

Supplementary Estimates are published throughout the year; each release is identified alphabetically (A, B, C, etc.). In general, Supplementary Estimates (A) are tabled in May and the associated appropriation act is granted royal assent and becomes law in June. These provide federal organizations with the funding they need early in the year. Supplementary Estimates (B) are tabled in late October or early November and the associated appropriation act is granted royal assent in December. Supplementary Estimates (C) are tabled in February and the associated appropriation act is granted royal assent in March.
notes_translated.fr:
Les budgets supplémentaires des dépenses font partie du processus normal d’approbation parlementaire qui permet de veiller à ce que les initiatives gouvernementales planifiées au préalable reçoivent le financement nécessaire pour aller de l’avant et répondre aux besoins des Canadiens. Ils présentent au Parlement de l’information sur les besoins en matière de dépenses du gouvernement du Canada qui n’étaient pas suffisamment élaborés au moment du dépôt du budget principal des dépenses, ou qui sont urgents mais imprévus, par exemple en cas de catastrophes naturelles. Les budgets supplémentaires des dépenses appuient directement une loi de crédits.

Les budgets supplémentaires des dépenses sont publiés au cours de l’exercice et désignés en ordre alphabétique (A, B, C, etc.). En général, le budget supplémentaire des dépenses (A) est déposé en mai, et la loi de crédits qui s’y rattache reçoit la sanction royale et devient loi en juin. Il fournit aux organisations fédérales les fonds dont elles ont besoin au début de l’année. Puis, le budget supplémentaire des dépenses (B) est déposé à la fin d’octobre ou au début de novembre, et la loi de crédits qui s’y rattache reçoit la sanction royale en décembre. Quant au budget supplémentaire des dépenses (C), il est déposé en février, et la loi de crédits qui s’y rattache reçoit la sanction royale en mars.

Supplementary Estimates (A) are tabled in May and the associated appropriation act is granted royal assent and becomes law in June. These provide federal organizations with the funding they need early in the year. Supplementary Estimates (B) are tabled in late October or early November and the associated appropriation act is granted royal assent in December. Supplementary Estimates (C) are tabled in February and the associated appropriation act is granted royal assent in March.

records.notes: Supplementary Estimates are part of the normal parliamentary approval process to ensure that previously planned government initiatives receive the necessary funding to move them forward, therefore meeting the needs of Canadians. They present information to Parliament on the Government of Canada’s spending requirements that were not sufficiently developed in time for inclusion in the Main Estimates, and will from time to time include urgent but unforeseen expenditures, such as natural disasters (an ice storm, H1N1, etc.). Supplementary Estimates directly support an appropriation act. Supplementary Estimates are published throughout the year; each release is identified alphabetically (A, B, C, etc.). Supplementary Estimates (A) are tabled in May and the associated appropriation act is granted royal assent and becomes law in June. These provide federal organizations with the funding they need early in the year. Supplementary Estimates (B) are tabled in late October or early November and the associated appropriation act is granted royal assent in December. Supplementary Estimates (C) are tabled in February and the associated appropriation act is granted royal assent in March.

notes_translated.fr: Le budget supplémentaire des dépenses fait partie du processus normal d'approbation parlementaire qui permet de veiller à ce que les initiatives gouvernementales planifiées au préalable reçoivent le financement nécessaire pour aller de l'avant et répondre aux besoins des Canadiens. Il présente au Parlement de l'information sur les besoins en matière de dépenses du gouvernement du Canada qui n'étaient pas suffisamment élaborées au moment du dépôt du budget principal des dépenses, et de temps à autres, il inclue des dépenses urgentes mais imprévues comme les dépenses engagées en cas de catastrophes naturelles (p. ex., une tempête de verglas et H1N1). Le budget supplémentaire des dépenses appuie directement une loi de crédits.
notes_translated.en: Supplementary Estimates are part of the normal parliamentary approval process to ensure that previously planned government initiatives receive the necessary funding to move them forward, therefore meeting the needs of Canadians. They present information to Parliament on the Government of Canada’s spending requirements that were not sufficiently developed in time for inclusion in the Main Estimates, and will from time to time include urgent but unforeseen expenditures, such as natural disasters (an ice storm, H1N1, etc.). Supplementary Estimates directly support an appropriation act. Supplementary Estimates are published throughout the year; each release is identified alphabetically (A, B, C, etc.). Supplementary Estimates (A) are tabled in May and the associated appropriation act is granted royal assent and becomes law in June. These provide federal organizations with the funding they need early in the year. Supplementary Estimates (B) are tabled in late October or early November and the associated appropriation act is granted royal assent in December. Supplementary Estimates (C) are tabled in February and the associated appropriation act is granted royal assent in March.

records.notes: Community organizations may be eligible for government community gaming grants to support their programs and services. Payments for approved grants are made throughout each government fiscal year, and the timing of the payments depends on the grant category / sector. This dataset includes information about community organizations in BC and the community gaming grant payments made to those organizations including: government fiscal year; city; organization name; grant type; grant sector; grant subsector; and payment amount.

notes_translated.en: Community organizations may be eligible for government community gaming grants to support their programs and services. Payments for approved grants are made throughout each government fiscal year, and the timing of the payments depends on the grant category / sector. This dataset includes information about community organizations in BC and the community gaming grant payments made to those organizations including: government fiscal year; city; organization name; grant type; grant sector; grant subsector; and payment amount.
notes_translated.fr-t-en: Les organisations communautaires peuvent être admissibles à des subventions gouvernementales pour les jeux communautaires afin de soutenir leurs programmes et services. Les paiements pour les subventions approuvées sont effectués tout au long de chaque exercice financier du gouvernement, et le calendrier des paiements dépend de la catégorie ou du secteur de subvention. Cet ensemble de données comprend des informations sur les organisations communautaires de la Colombie-Britannique et les paiements de subventions aux jeux communautaires versés à ces organisations, notamment : l'exercice financier du gouvernement, la ville, le nom de l'organisation, le type de subvention, le secteur de subvention, le sous-secteur des subventions et le montant des paiements.

records.name: 7be8a389-fae5-4193-ab54-71d82b135423
records.notes: The Strategic Jobs and Investment Fund was a grant and loan program that attracted investment in leading-edge projects that built strategic capacity and created new, high-value-added jobs. SJIF targeted innovative companies that made anchor investments, and supported cluster development within Ontario. This dataset contains a list of recipients of Strategic Jobs and Investment Fund from 2010 to 2015. This list includes the following details: * funding program * name of company * location * fiscal year contract signed * government funding commitment * total project jobs created and retained as in the contract.

notes_translated.en: The Strategic Jobs and Investment Fund was a grant and loan program that attracted investment in leading-edge projects that built strategic capacity and created new, high-value-added jobs. SJIF targeted innovative companies that made anchor investments, and supported cluster development within Ontario. This dataset contains a list of recipients of Strategic Jobs and Investment Fund from 2010 to 2015. This list includes the following details: * funding program * name of company * location * fiscal year contract signed * government funding commitment * total project jobs created and retained as in the contract.
notes_translated.fr: Le Fonds pour les emplois et les investissements strategiques etait un programme de prets et de subventions qui avait pour objectif d'attirer l'investissement dans les projets de pointe visant a consolider la capacite strategique et a creer de nouveaux emplois a grande valeur ajoutee. Le FEIS privilegiait les entreprises d'innovation qui faisaient des investissements strategiques et soutenaient le developpement de regroupements en Ontario. Cet ensemble de donnees comprend une liste des beneficiaires du Fonds pour les emplois et les investissements strategiques de 2010 a 2015\. Cette liste contient les renseignements suivants : * le programme de financement; * le nom de l'entreprise; * le lieu; * l'exercice de signature du contrat; * l'engagement financier du gouvernement; * le nombre total d'emplois crees et conserves dans le cadre du contrat.

records.name: e4b7f97b-257a-4ea3-b364-7dfac64992f9
records.notes:
Supplementary Estimates are part of the normal parliamentary approval process to ensure that previously planned government initiatives receive the necessary funding to move them forward, therefore meeting the needs of Canadians. They present information to Parliament on the Government of Canada’s spending requirements that were not sufficiently developed in time for inclusion in the Main Estimates, and will from time to time include urgent but unforeseen expenditures, such as natural disasters (an ice storm, H1N1, etc.). Supplementary Estimates directly support an appropriation act.

Supplementary Estimates are published throughout the year; each release is identified alphabetically (A, B, C, etc.). Supplementary Estimates (A) are tabled in May and the associated appropriation act is granted royal assent and becomes law in June. These provide federal organizations with the funding they need early in the year. Supplementary Estimates (B) are tabled in late October or early November and the associated appropriation act is granted royal assent in December. Supplementary Estimates (C) are tabled in February and the associated appropriation act is granted royal assent in March.

notes_translated.fr:
Le budget supplémentaire des dépenses fait partie du processus normal d’approbation parlementaire qui permet de veiller à ce que les initiatives gouvernementales planifiées au préalable reçoivent le financement nécessaire pour aller de l’avant et répondre aux besoins des Canadiens. Il présente au Parlement de l’information sur les besoins en matière de dépenses du gouvernement du Canada qui n’étaient pas suffisamment élaborées au moment du dépôt du budget principal des dépenses, et de temps à autres, il inclue des dépenses urgentes mais imprévues comme les dépenses engagées en cas de catastrophes naturelles (p. ex., une tempête de verglas et H1N1). Le budget supplémentaire des dépenses appuie directement une loi de crédits.

Le budget supplémentaire des dépenses est publié au cours de l’exercice. Chaque publication est désignée en ordre alphabétique (A, B, C, etc.) Le budget supplémentaire des dépenses (A) est déposé en mai, et la loi de crédits qui s’y rattache reçoit la sanction royale et devient loi en juin. Il fournit aux organisations fédérales les fonds dont elles ont besoin au début de l’année. Le budget supplémentaire des dépenses (B) est déposé à la fin octobre ou au début de novembre, et la loi de crédits qui s’y rattache reçoit la sanction royale en décembre. Le budget supplémentaire des dépenses (C) est déposé en février, et la loi de crédits qui s’y rattache reçoit la sanction royale en mars.
notes_translated.en:

They present information to Parliament on the Government of Canada’s spending requirements that were not sufficiently developed in time for inclusion in the Main Estimates, and will from time to time include urgent but unforeseen expenditures, such as natural disasters (an ice storm, H1N1, etc.). Supplementary Estimates directly support an appropriation act.

Supplementary Estimates are published throughout the year; each release is identified alphabetically (A, B, C, etc.). Supplementary Estimates (A) are tabled in May and the associated appropriation act is granted royal assent and becomes law in June. These provide federal organizations with the funding they need early in the year. Supplementary Estimates (B) are tabled in late October or early November and the associated appropriation act is granted royal assent in December. Supplementary Estimates (C) are tabled in February and the associated appropriation act is granted royal assent in March.

records.notes: The Inclusive Community Grants Program provides funding to local governments, not-for-profit organizations and Indigenous communities/organizations. The goal of the program is to encourage local government and community organizations to consider Ontarians of all ages and abilities at every stage of community planning and development. The dataset includes the following information: * Project name * Description of program/service to be funded * Name of Organization * Location of Organization * Amount of Grant

notes_translated.en: The Inclusive Community Grants Program provides funding to local governments, not-for-profit organizations and Indigenous communities/organizations. The goal of the program is to encourage local government and community organizations to consider Ontarians of all ages and abilities at every stage of community planning and development. The dataset includes the following information: * Project name * Description of program/service to be funded * Name of Organization * Location of Organization * Amount of Grant
notes_translated.fr: Le Programme de subventions pour des collectivités inclusives offre du financement aux administrations locales, aux organismes sans but lucratif et aux collectivités et aux organisations autochtones. L’objectif du programme est d’encourager les administrations locales et les organismes communautaires à tenir compte des Ontariens de tous âges et de toutes capacités à chaque étape de la planification et du développement communautaires. L’ensemble de données comprend les renseignements suivants : * Nom du projet * Description du programme/service à financer * Nom de l’organisation * Lieu de l’organisation * Montant de la subventio

fr: IACNC - Assistance en Capital pour les projets clôturés

records.name: cad72695-257e-4b13-adc4-802cf044d794
records.notes:
From 2019 to 2022, the Supporting Black Canadian Communities Initiative (SBCCI) played a crucial role in bolstering Black-led, Black-focused, and Black-serving (B3) nonprofit organizations across Canada through its Capital Assistance funding program. This initiative provided financial support to address critical infrastructure and equipment needs, thereby enhancing the capacity of these organizations to serve their communities effectively. The grants enabled B3 organizations to undertake minor renovations or retrofits of their facilities and to purchase essential equipment.

The program’s primary objectives were to:

1) Enhance Organizational Capacity: By improving infrastructure and acquiring necessary equipment, B3 
 organizations were better equipped to fulfill their missions and manage their operations more efficiently.

2) Improve Service Delivery: With enhanced facilities and resources, these organizations could offer better services to Black communities across Canada.

Organizations eligible for the program could apply for grants of up to $100,000. These funds were allocated for two main types of improvements:

- Equipment Purchases (e.g., upgrading computers or software): To equip workspaces and community spaces are identified by red points on the attached map.

- Renovations/Retrofits: To undertake improvements or modifications to existing facilities are indicated by green points on the map.

- Both Equipment and Renovations/Retrofits (e.g., renovating community kitchens to increase capacity): Projects that include both types of funding are represented by blue points on the map.

The dataset provided focuses exclusively on projects that have been completed and closed. It does not include projects that are still ongoing, those that were found ineligible for funding, or projects that could not be finished.

notes_translated.en:
From 2019 to 2022, the Supporting Black Canadian Communities Initiative (SBCCI) played a crucial role in bolstering Black-led, Black-focused, and Black-serving (B3) nonprofit organizations across Canada through its Capital Assistance funding program. This initiative provided financial support to address critical infrastructure and equipment needs, thereby enhancing the capacity of these organizations to serve their communities effectively. The grants enabled B3 organizations to undertake minor renovations or retrofits of their facilities and to purchase essential equipment.

The program’s primary objectives were to:

1) Enhance Organizational Capacity: By improving infrastructure and acquiring necessary equipment, B3 
 organizations were better equipped to fulfill their missions and manage their operations more efficiently.

2) Improve Service Delivery: With enhanced facilities and resources, these organizations could offer better services to Black communities across Canada.

Organizations eligible for the program could apply for grants of up to $100,000. These funds were allocated for two main types of improvements:

- Equipment Purchases (e.g., upgrading computers or software): To equip workspaces and community spaces are identified by red points on the attached map.

- Renovations/Retrofits: To undertake improvements or modifications to existing facilities are indicated by green points on the map.

- Both Equipment and Renovations/Retrofits (e.g., renovating community kitchens to increase capacity): Projects that include both types of funding are represented by blue points on the map.

The dataset provided focuses exclusively on projects that have been completed and closed. It does not include projects that are still ongoing, those that were found ineligible for funding, or projects that could not be finished.
notes_translated.fr:
De 2019 à 2022, l'Initiative Appuyer les Communautés Noires du Canada (IACNC)  a joué un rôle clé en renforçant les organisations à but non lucratif dirigées, ciblées et servant des personnes noires (B3) au Canada grâce à son programme de financement en capital. Cette initiative a fourni un soutien financier pour répondre à des besoins critiques en infrastructures et en équipements, permettant ainsi à ces organisations d'améliorer leur capacité à servir efficacement leurs communautés. Les subventions ont permis aux organisations B3 de réaliser des rénovations mineures ou des réaménagements de leurs installations et d'acheter des équipements essentiels.

Les principaux objectifs du programme étaient de :

1) Renforcer la Capacité Organisationnelle : En améliorant les infrastructures et en acquérant les équipements nécessaires, les organisations B3 ont pu mieux accomplir leurs missions et gérer leurs opérations plus efficacement.

2) Améliorer la Prestation de Services : Avec des installations et des ressources améliorées, ces organisations ont pu offrir de meilleurs services aux communautés noires à travers le Canada.

Les organisations éligibles pouvaient demander des subventions allant jusqu'à 100 000 $. Ces fonds pouvaient être utilisés pour deux types principaux d'améliorations :

- Achats d'Équipements (p. ex., mise à niveau des ordinateurs ou logiciels) : Pour équiper les espaces de travail et communautaires sont identifiés par des points rouges sur la carte jointe.

- Rénovations/Réaménagements : Pour réaliser des améliorations ou des modifications des installations existantes sont indiquées par des points verts sur la carte.

- Achats d'Équipements et Rénovations/Réaménagements (p. ex., rénovation des cuisines communautaires pour augmenter la capacité) : Les projets incluant ces deux types de financement sont représentés par des points bleus sur la carte.

Les données fourni se concentre exclusivement sur les projets terminés et clôturés. Il ne comprend pas les projets encore en cours, ceux jugés inéligibles pour le financement, ou les projets qui n'ont pas pu être achevés.

records.name: dd46d9bc-dc33-4284-bdf4-1e19cfd3edc0
records.notes: Strategic Investments (funded through the Strategic Jobs and Investment Fund) was a grant and loan program that attracted investment in leading-edge projects that built strategic capacity and created new, high-value-added jobs. SI targeted innovative companies that made anchor investments, and supported cluster development within Ontario. This dataset also includes some projects from the older programs for Large Scale Strategic Investments (LSSI) and Strategic Manufacturing Investments (SMI). This data set contains a list of recipients of Strategic Investment for 2011-12, and all projects from the older LSSI and SMI programs. This list includes the following details: * funding program * name of company * location * fiscal year contract signed * government funding commitment * total project jobs created and retained as in the contract.

notes_translated.en: Strategic Investments (funded through the Strategic Jobs and Investment Fund) was a grant and loan program that attracted investment in leading-edge projects that built strategic capacity and created new, high-value-added jobs. SI targeted innovative companies that made anchor investments, and supported cluster development within Ontario. This dataset also includes some projects from the older programs for Large Scale Strategic Investments (LSSI) and Strategic Manufacturing Investments (SMI). This data set contains a list of recipients of Strategic Investment for 2011-12, and all projects from the older LSSI and SMI programs. This list includes the following details: * funding program * name of company * location * fiscal year contract signed * government funding commitment * total project jobs created and retained as in the contract.
notes_translated.fr: L'investissement strategique (finance par le Fonds pour les emplois et les investissements strategiques) etait un programme de prets et de subventions qui avait pour objectif d'attirer l'investissement dans les projets de pointe visant a consolider la capacite strategique et a creer de nouveaux emplois a grande valeur ajoutee. L'IS privilegiait les entreprises d'innovation qui faisaient des investissements strategiques et soutenaient le developpement de regroupements en Ontario. Cet ensemble de donnees comprend egalement certains projets de l'ancien Fonds d'investissements strategiques dans les grands projets et de l'ancienne initiative Investissement strategique dans le secteur manufacturier. Cet ensemble de donnees contient une liste des beneficiaires de l'investissement strategique pour 2011 et 2012 et tous les projets des deux anciens programmes d'investissement. Cette liste contient les renseignements suivants : * le programme de financement; * le nom de l'entreprise; * le lieu; * l'exercice de signature du contrat; * l'engagement financier du gouvernement; * le nombre total d'emplois crees et conserves dans le cadre du contrat.

records.name: 60263d0f-5bd5-492a-81de-d6f7dc35c7e4
records.notes: Weighted average tuition and additional compulsory fees for full-time Canadian undergraduate and graduate students. Additional compulsory fees apply to all Canadian students, regardless of the field of study. Typically, they include fees for athletics, student health services and student associations. Data are collected from all publicly funded Canadian degree-granting institutions.

notes_translated.en: Weighted average tuition and additional compulsory fees for full-time Canadian undergraduate and graduate students. Additional compulsory fees apply to all Canadian students, regardless of the field of study. Typically, they include fees for athletics, student health services and student associations. Data are collected from all publicly funded Canadian degree-granting institutions.
notes_translated.fr: Moyennes pondérées des droits de scolarité et des frais supplémentaires obligatoires des étudiants canadiens à temps plein de premier cycle et de cycles supérieurs. Les frais supplémentaires obligatoires s'appliquent à tous les étudiants canadiens, indépendamment de leur domaine d'études. Ils comprennent généralement les frais pour les activités sportives, les services de santé offerts aux étudiants et les associations étudiantes. Les données sont recueillies auprès de l’ensemble des établissements canadiens qui confèrent des grades et qui sont subventionnés par des fonds publics.

records.name: c1618876-a48e-41d0-ab30-31d798aaa075
records.notes:
Supplementary Estimates are part of the normal parliamentary approval process to ensure that previously planned government initiatives receive the necessary funding to move them forward, therefore meeting the needs of Canadians. They present information to Parliament on the Government of Canada’s spending requirements that were not sufficiently developed in time for inclusion in the Main Estimates, or urgent but unforeseen, such as natural disasters. Supplementary Estimates directly support an appropriation act.

Supplementary Estimates are published throughout the year; each release is identified alphabetically (A, B, C, etc.). In general, Supplementary Estimates (A) are tabled in May and the associated appropriation act is granted royal assent and becomes law in June. These provide federal organizations with the funding they need early in the year. Supplementary Estimates (B) are tabled in late October or early November and the associated appropriation act is granted royal assent in December. Supplementary Estimates (C) are tabled in February and the associated appropriation act is granted royal assent in March.

notes_translated.fr:
Les budgets supplémentaires des dépenses font partie du processus normal d’approbation parlementaire qui permet de veiller à ce que les initiatives gouvernementales planifiées au préalable reçoivent le financement nécessaire pour aller de l’avant et répondre aux besoins des Canadiens. Ils présentent au Parlement de l’information sur les besoins en matière de dépenses du gouvernement du Canada qui n’étaient pas suffisamment élaborés au moment du dépôt du budget principal des dépenses, ou qui sont urgents mais imprévus, par exemple en cas de catastrophes naturelles. Les budgets supplémentaires des dépenses appuient directement une loi de crédits.

Les budgets supplémentaires des dépenses sont publiés au cours de l’exercice et désignés en ordre alphabétique (A, B, C, etc.). En général, le budget supplémentaire des dépenses (A) est déposé en mai, et la loi de crédits qui s’y rattache reçoit la sanction royale et devient loi en juin. Il fournit aux organisations fédérales les fonds dont elles ont besoin au début de l’année. Puis, le budget supplémentaire des dépenses (B) est déposé à la fin d’octobre ou au début de novembre, et la loi de crédits qui s’y rattache reçoit la sanction royale en décembre. Quant au budget supplémentaire des dépenses (C), il est déposé en février, et la loi de crédits qui s’y rattache reçoit la sanction royale en mars.
notes_translated.en:
Supplementary Estimates are part of the normal parliamentary approval process to ensure that previously planned government initiatives receive the necessary funding to move them forward, therefore meeting the needs of Canadians. They present information to Parliament on the Government of Canada’s spending requirements that were not sufficiently developed in time for inclusion in the Main Estimates, or urgent but unforeseen, such as natural disasters. Supplementary Estimates directly support an appropriation act.

Supplementary Estimates are published throughout the year; each release is identified alphabetically (A, B, C, etc.). In general, Supplementary Estimates (A) are tabled in May and the associated appropriation act is granted royal assent and becomes law in June. These provide federal organizations with the funding they need early in the year. Supplementary Estimates (B) are tabled in late October or early November and the associated appropriation act is granted royal assent in December. Supplementary Estimates (C) are tabled in February and the associated appropriation act is granted royal assent in March.

records.name: 670a4911-0248-48ee-9071-b8793ed653a4
records.notes:
The Government Affairs Unit has reviewed the briefs for the SRSR study Use of Federal
Government Research and Development Grants, Funds, and Contributions by Canadian
Universities and Research Institutions in Partnerships with Entities Connected to the People’s
Republic of China.

notes_translated.fr:
L’Unité des affaires gouvernementales a examiné les mémoires soumis pour l’étude du Comité
permanent de la science et de la recherche de la Chambre des communes intitulée L’utilisation
des subventions, fonds et contributions du gouvernement fédéral en matière de recherche et de
développement par les universités et les établissements de recherche canadiens dans le cadre de
partenariat avec des entités liées à la République populaire de Chine.
notes_translated.en:
The Government Affairs Unit has reviewed the briefs for the SRSR study Use of Federal
Government Research and Development Grants, Funds, and Contributions by Canadian
Universities and Research Institutions in Partnerships with Entities Connected to the People’s
Republic of China.

records.name: 5b17d434-5e86-49bb-a839-9d5b4202bf01
records.notes: The Multicultural Community Capacity Grant Program promotes diversity and inclusion and supports civic and community engagement among newcomer and ethno-cultural communities by providing funding to projects run by community groups. Data includes: * applicant stream * organization and representative names * location (city or town) * project priorities The grants are awarded to projects that focus on one or more of the following priorities: 1. civic engagement 2. social connections 3. education and empowerment 4. women's empowerment 5. capacity building and partnerships Applications for grants were separated into two streams: 1. unincorporated organizations represented by a person 2. incorporated organizations [Learn more about the Multicultural Community Capacity Grant Program](/page /multicultural-community-capacity-grant-program)

notes_translated.en: The Multicultural Community Capacity Grant Program promotes diversity and inclusion and supports civic and community engagement among newcomer and ethno-cultural communities by providing funding to projects run by community groups. Data includes: * applicant stream * organization and representative names * location (city or town) * project priorities The grants are awarded to projects that focus on one or more of the following priorities: 1. civic engagement 2. social connections 3. education and empowerment 4. women's empowerment 5. capacity building and partnerships Applications for grants were separated into two streams: 1. unincorporated organizations represented by a person 2. incorporated organizations [Learn more about the Multicultural Community Capacity Grant Program](/page /multicultural-community-capacity-grant-program)
notes_translated.fr: Le Programme de subventions de renforcement des capacites pour la communaute multiculturelle favorise la diversite et l'inclusion et appuie l'engagement civique et communautaire des nouveaux arrivants et des communautes ethnoculturelles en finançant des projets diriges par des groupes communautaires. Les donnees comprennent : * le volet s'appliquant a l'organisme demandeur * le nom de l'organisme et du/de la representant(e) * le lieu (ville) * les priorites du projet Les subventions sont attribuees a des projets axes sur une ou plusieurs des priorites suivantes : 1. engagement civique 2. liens sociaux 3. education et habilitation 4. habilitation des femmes 5. developpement des capacites et partenariats Les demandes de subventions sont reparties en deux volets : 1. organismes non-constitues representes par une personne 2. organismes constitues

They present information to Parliament on the Government of Canada’s spending requirements that were not sufficiently developed in time for inclusion in the Main Estimates, and will from time to time include urgent but unforeseen expenditures, such as natural disasters (an ice storm, H1N1, etc.). Supplementary Estimates directly support an appropriation act.

Supplementary Estimates are published throughout the year; each release is identified alphabetically (A, B, C, etc.). Supplementary Estimates (A) are tabled in May and the associated appropriation act is granted royal assent and becomes law in June. These provide federal organizations with the funding they need early in the year. Supplementary Estimates (B) are tabled in late October or early November and the associated appropriation act is granted royal assent in December. Supplementary Estimates (C) are tabled in February and the associated appropriation act is granted royal assent in March.

records.notes: The Innovation Demonstration Fund was designed to help companies develop promising green technologies and get them to market by focusing on pilot-scale technology demonstration projects. The IDF has been on pause since September 10, 2013. This data set contains a list of recipients of Innovation Demonstration Fund from 2007 to 2015. This list includes the following details: * funding program * name of company * location * fiscal year contract signed * government funding commitment.

notes_translated.en: The Innovation Demonstration Fund was designed to help companies develop promising green technologies and get them to market by focusing on pilot-scale technology demonstration projects. The IDF has been on pause since September 10, 2013. This data set contains a list of recipients of Innovation Demonstration Fund from 2007 to 2015. This list includes the following details: * funding program * name of company * location * fiscal year contract signed * government funding commitment.
notes_translated.fr: Le Fonds pour les projets pilotes d'innovation avait ete conçu pour aider les entreprises a mettre au point des technologies vertes et a les commercialiser a l'aide de projets pilotes permettant une demonstration technologique. Le FPPI est en pause depuis le 10 septembre 2013. Cet ensemble de donnees comprend une liste des beneficiaires du Fonds pour les projets pilotes d'innovation de 2007 a 2015\. Cette liste contient les renseignements suivants : * le programme de financement; * le nom de l'entreprise; * le lieu; * l'exercice de signature du contrat; * l'engagement financier du gouvernement.

records.name: 5200adaa-075d-458d-b384-33c1a807cd74
records.notes: Additional compulsory fees are weighted averages for full-time Canadian undergraduate and graduate students. These fees apply to all Canadian students, regardless of field of study. Typically, they include fees for athletics, student health services and student associations. Data is collected from all publicly funded Canadian degree-granting institutions.

notes_translated.fr: Les frais supplémentaires obligatoires sont des moyennes pondérées des étudiants canadiens à temps plein de premier cycle et des cycles supérieurs. Ces frais s'appliquent à tous les étudiants canadiens, indépendamment de leur domaine d'études. Ils comprennent généralement les frais pour les activités sportives, les services de santé offerts aux étudiants et les associations étudiantes. Les données sont recueillies auprès de l'ensemble des établissements canadiens qui confèrent des grades et qui sont subventionnés par des fonds publics.
notes_translated.en: Additional compulsory fees are weighted averages for full-time Canadian undergraduate and graduate students. These fees apply to all Canadian students, regardless of field of study. Typically, they include fees for athletics, student health services and student associations. Data is collected from all publicly funded Canadian degree-granting institutions.

records.name: bf95788f-e27f-4d1f-a850-97f5f36de480
records.notes: Get data on grants, recipients and project information distributed by the Ministry of Labour, Training and Skills Development under a number of research programs. This dataset details funding provided to recipients under the Bridging the Gap, Research Opportunities Program, Applied Research Questions, and the Occupational Health & Safety Prevention and Innovation programs. These grants aim to improve occupational health and safety outcomes by supporting projects that reduce workplace injuries, illnesses and fatalities. Details include: * year of grant application * grant program * approved funding total * receiving organization * project title, contact, investigator and partner organization * titles of articles and products produced * project abstract and introduction The [Bridging the Gap](https://www.wsib.ca/en/wsibs-former-rac-research-program-1998-2012) program focused on addressing workplace occupational health and safety needs. It supported partnerships among workplaces, occupational health and safety organizations, and researchers to generate evidence and evidence-based products that addressed workplace needs. The [Research Opportunities Program](https://www.labour.gov.on.ca/english/hs/prevention/research_proposals.php)’s objective is to invest in research and knowledge dissemination projects that focus on occupational health and safety system priorities. The Applied Research Questions grant generated evidence that informed the implementation of the occupational health and safety system priorities. The [Occupational Health and Safety Prevention and Innovation Program](https://www.labour.gov.on.ca/english/hs/prevention/innovation_program.php)’s objective is to support workplace-focused innovation projects and collaborative partnerships that lead to improvements in occupational health and safety in Ontario. Please contact researchgrants@ontario.ca if you require an alternate format or for any questions. Please be advised that some of the links available in the dataset lead to English only websites

notes_translated.fr: Obtenez des renseignements sur les subventions, les bénéficiaires et les projets diffusés par le ministère du Travail, de la Formation et du Développement des compétences dans le cadre de plusieurs programmes de recherche. Cet ensemble de renseignements détaille le financement fourni aux bénéficiaires dans le cadre du programme Combler les lacunes, du Programme des perspectives de recherche, des Questions de recherche appliquée et du Programme d’innovation pour la prévention, la santé et la sécurité au travail. Ces subventions visent à améliorer les résultats en matière de santé et de sécurité au travail en favorisant des projets qui réduisent les blessures, les maladies et les décès qui surviennent au travail. Des précisions sont fournies à l’égard de ce qui suit : * l’année de la demande de subvention; * le programme de subventions; * le financement total approuvé; * l’organisme destinataire; * le titre du projet, les coordonnées du chercheur et l’organisme partenaire; * les titres des articles et produits fabriqués; * le résumé et l’introduction du projet. Le programme [Combler les lacunes](https://www.wsib.ca/fr/ancien-programme-recherche-du-ccr-cspaat-1998-2012) visait à répondre aux besoins en matière de santé et de sécurité au travail. Il a soutenu des partenariats entre le milieu de travail, les organismes de santé et de sécurité au travail et les chercheurs afin de générer des éléments probants et des produits fondés sur des données probantes qui répondent aux besoins des milieux de travail. L’objectif du [Programme des perspectives de recherche](https://www.labour.gov.on.ca/french/hs/prevention/research_proposals.php) consiste à investir dans des projets de recherche ou de diffusion des connaissances axés sur les priorités du système de santé et de sécurité au travail. La subvention pour les Questions de recherche appliquée a généré des données probantes qui ont documenté la mise en œuvre des priorités du système de santé et de sécurité au travail. L’objectif du [Programme d’innovation pour la prévention, la santé et la sécurité au travail](https://www.labour.gov.on.ca/french/hs/prevention/innovation_program.php) est d’appuyer les projets d’innovation axés sur le milieu de travail et les partenariats de collaboration qui apportent des améliorations à la santé et à la sécurité au travail en Ontario. Si vous avez besoin d’un autre format ou si vous avez des questions à nous soumettre, veuillez écrire par courriel à researchgrants@ontario.ca. Veuillez noter que certains liens dans l'ensemble de renseignements mènent à des sites Web uniquement en anglais.

records.notes: Child and youth mental health services provide a range of planned, multidisciplinary interventions for youth and their families. In each defined service area, a designated Transfer Payment Agency is responsible for coordinating ministry-funded child and youth mental health services. The agency also ensures the defined core services and key processes outlined in the Child and Youth Mental Health Service Framework are implemented in all service providers.

notes_translated.en: Child and youth mental health services provide a range of planned, multidisciplinary interventions for youth and their families. In each defined service area, a designated Transfer Payment Agency is responsible for coordinating ministry-funded child and youth mental health services. The agency also ensures the defined core services and key processes outlined in the Child and Youth Mental Health Service Framework are implemented in all service providers.
notes_translated.fr: Les services de sante mentale aux enfants et aux jeunes offrent une gamme d'interventions planifiees et pluridisciplinaires aux jeunes et a leur famille. Dans chaque zone de service definie, un organisme beneficiaire de paiements de transfert designe se charge de coordonner les services de sante mentale aux enfants et aux jeunes finances par le ministere. L'organisme veille en outre a ce que tous les fournisseurs de services mettent en oeuvre les services de base et les principaux processus definis dans le Cadre de prestation des services de sante mentale aux enfants et aux jeunes.

records.name: 54e26c02-890c-c816-a049-92e9da0ee30d
records.notes: The Emergency Services Provider Fund is a program intended to assist emergency first responder organizations in Nova Scotia with the purchase of equipment used directly in response to a fire or emergency situation. The program will provide up to 75 per cent of the total capital cost up to a maximum grant of $20,000. This dataset includes the list of the providers that were approved, the inital project approval amount and the actual amount paid to each applicant based on the finished project. Funding for this program was last approved in 2018-19 budget.

notes_translated.en: The Emergency Services Provider Fund is a program intended to assist emergency first responder organizations in Nova Scotia with the purchase of equipment used directly in response to a fire or emergency situation. The program will provide up to 75 per cent of the total capital cost up to a maximum grant of $20,000. This dataset includes the list of the providers that were approved, the inital project approval amount and the actual amount paid to each applicant based on the finished project. Funding for this program was last approved in 2018-19 budget.
notes_translated.fr-t-en: Le Fonds pour les fournisseurs de services d'urgence est un programme destiné à aider les organisations de premiers intervenants d'urgence de la Nouvelle-Écosse à acheter de l'équipement utilisé directement en réponse à un incendie ou à une situation d'urgence. Le programme couvrira jusqu'à 75 % du coût total en capital, jusqu'à une subvention maximale de 20 000$. Cet ensemble de données comprend la liste des fournisseurs approuvés, le montant initial approuvé pour le projet et le montant réel payé à chaque demandeur sur la base du projet terminé. Le financement de ce programme a été approuvé pour la dernière fois dans le budget de 2018-2019.

records.name: c0f5f4fe-e3ba-4f4e-a587-797c89774f79
records.notes: Tuition and additional compulsory fees are weighted averages for full-time Canadian undergraduate and graduate students. Additional compulsory fees apply to all Canadian students, regardless of field of study. Typically, they include fees for athletics, student health services and student associations. Data is collected from all publicly funded Canadian degree-granting institutions.

notes_translated.fr: Les droits de scolarité et les frais supplémentaires obligatoires sont des moyennes pondérées des étudiants canadiens à temps plein de premier cycle et des cycles supérieurs. Les frais supplémentaires obligatoires s'appliquent à tous les étudiants canadiens, indépendamment de leur domaine d'études. Ils comprennent généralement les frais pour les activités sportives, les services de santé offerts aux étudiants et les associations étudiantes. Les données sont recueillies auprès de l'ensemble des établissements canadiens qui confèrent des grades et qui sont subventionnés par des fonds publics.
notes_translated.en: Tuition and additional compulsory fees are weighted averages for full-time Canadian undergraduate and graduate students. Additional compulsory fees apply to all Canadian students, regardless of field of study. Typically, they include fees for athletics, student health services and student associations. Data is collected from all publicly funded Canadian degree-granting institutions.

records.name: 0bc36c69-52f2-433b-8433-063cd81e0ce7
records.notes: The Next Generation of Jobs Fund was announced in March 2008 as one of the cornerstones of the government's economic development strategy to stimulate economic growth and create a sustainable and globally competitive workplace for the next generation of workers. The purpose of the fund was to support research, development and commercialization in innovative industries and attract new investments in strategic areas of Ontario's economy. The Next Generation of Jobs Fund has stopped accepting new applications, but the program is actively managing existing contracts. This data set contains a list of recipients of Next Generation of Jobs Fund from 2008 to 2012. This list includes the following details: * funding program * name of company * location * fiscal year contract signed * government funding commitment * total project jobs created and retained as in the contract.

notes_translated.en: The Next Generation of Jobs Fund was announced in March 2008 as one of the cornerstones of the government's economic development strategy to stimulate economic growth and create a sustainable and globally competitive workplace for the next generation of workers. The purpose of the fund was to support research, development and commercialization in innovative industries and attract new investments in strategic areas of Ontario's economy. The Next Generation of Jobs Fund has stopped accepting new applications, but the program is actively managing existing contracts. This data set contains a list of recipients of Next Generation of Jobs Fund from 2008 to 2012. This list includes the following details: * funding program * name of company * location * fiscal year contract signed * government funding commitment * total project jobs created and retained as in the contract.
notes_translated.fr: Le Fonds pour les emplois dans les secteurs emergents a ete lance en mars 2008\. Il representait alors l'une des pierres angulaires de la strategie de developpement economique du gouvernement pour stimuler la croissance economique et creer des lieux de travail durables et competitifs sur la scene mondiale pour la prochaine generation de travailleurs. L'objectif du fonds etait de soutenir la recherche, le developpement et la commercialisation dans les industries innovantes, et d'attirer de nouveaux investissements dans des secteurs strategiques de l'economie ontarienne. Le Fonds pour les emplois dans les secteurs emergents n'accepte plus de demandes, mais le programme poursuit la gestion des contrats toujours en vigueur. Cet ensemble de donnees comprend une liste des beneficiaires du Fonds pour les emplois dans les secteurs emergents de 2008 a 2012\. Cette liste contient les renseignements suivants : * le programme de financement; * le nom de l'entreprise; * le lieu; * l'exercice de signature du contrat; * l'engagement financier du gouvernement; * le nombre total d'emplois crees et conserves dans le cadre du contrat.

records.name: 4dbe2803-4454-4c73-8a1e-268a3794e4b7
records.notes:
This report presents the findings of the evaluation of the Partnerships Contribution Program (PCP), which covers the period between April 2008 and March 2011. This evaluation meets the Treasury Board Secretariat's requirement for evaluation coverage of all ongoing programs of grants and contributions. An evaluation of the PCP is also required as part of the renewal of the Terms and Conditions.

The PCP provides a means through which Veterans Affairs Canada (VAC) can extend its reach in delivering its mandate regarding commemorative activities. The program provides non-repayable contributions to a range of potential recipients, such as non-profit organizations, museums, educational institutions, provinces, territories and municipalities. There are two funding opportunities available under the PCP:

  * the Community Engagement Partnership Fund (CEPF) provides eligible organizations and groups with financial assistance to deliver remembrance activities and events regionally and nationally; and

  * the Cenotaph/Monument Restoration Program (CMRP) provides eligible organizations and groups with financial assistance to properly conserve cenotaphs and monuments honouring Canada's war dead and Veterans.

notes_translated.en:
This report presents the findings of the evaluation of the Partnerships Contribution Program (PCP), which covers the period between April 2008 and March 2011. This evaluation meets the Treasury Board Secretariat's requirement for evaluation coverage of all ongoing programs of grants and contributions. An evaluation of the PCP is also required as part of the renewal of the Terms and Conditions.

The PCP provides a means through which Veterans Affairs Canada (VAC) can extend its reach in delivering its mandate regarding commemorative activities. The program provides non-repayable contributions to a range of potential recipients, such as non-profit organizations, museums, educational institutions, provinces, territories and municipalities. There are two funding opportunities available under the PCP:

  * the Community Engagement Partnership Fund (CEPF) provides eligible organizations and groups with financial assistance to deliver remembrance activities and events regionally and nationally; and

  * the Cenotaph/Monument Restoration Program (CMRP) provides eligible organizations and groups with financial assistance to properly conserve cenotaphs and monuments honouring Canada's war dead and Veterans.
notes_translated.fr:
Le présent rapport fait état des résultats de l'évaluation du Programme des contributions aux partenaires (PCP), qui visait la période d'avril 2008 à mars 2011. Cette évaluation répond à l'exigence du Secrétariat du Conseil du Trésor voulant que tous les programmes permanents de subventions et de contributions soient évalués. En outre, une évaluation du PCP est requise dans le cadre du renouvellement de ses paramètres.

Le PCP permet à Anciens Combattants Canada (ACC) d'accroître sa présence en remplissant son mandat relativement aux activités de commémoration. Dans le cadre du programme, des contributions non remboursables sont versées à un éventail de bénéficiaires éventuels, comme des organismes sans but lucratif, des musées, des établissements d'enseignement, des provinces, des territoires et des municipalités. Le PCP offre deux possibilités de financement :

  * le Fonds de partenariat d'action communautaire (FPAC), qui offre une aide financière aux groupes et aux organismes admissibles pour la réalisation d'activités commémoratives à l'échelle régionale et nationale;

  * le Programme de restauration de cénotaphes et de monuments (PRCM), qui offre une aide financière aux groupes et aux organismes admissibles afin de conserver de manière adéquate les cénotaphes et monuments érigés à la mémoire des soldats canadiens morts à la guerre, des anciens combattants et des vétérans.

records.notes: The report provides an annual overview of activities undertaken by SSHRC program portfolios, and incorporates feedback from committee members and observers. Point-in-time data on applications adjudicated and awarded during the 2023-24 fiscal year is included to inform and support SSHRC's Programs Committee discussions. These findings also serve to inform continuous improvement for SSHRC programs.

notes_translated.en: The report provides an annual overview of activities undertaken by SSHRC program portfolios, and incorporates feedback from committee members and observers. notes_translated.fr: Ce rapport constitue un bilan annuel des activités menées  par les portefeuilles des programmes du CRSH au cours de l’exercice et comprend les commentaires des membres de comité et des observateurs. Les données temporelles sur les demandes sélectionnées et acceptées au cours de l’exercice financier 2023-2024 sont également fournies pour éclairer et soutenir les discussions du Comité des programmes du CRSH. Ces conclusions servent aussi à éclairer les programmes du CRSH en vue de leur amélioration.

data_series_name.fr: Évaluation après la campagne publicitaire relative aux prestations financières pour les familles canadiennes
data_series_name.en: Post-campaign evaluation of the financial benefits for Canadian families advertising campaign

records.name: bb7c61c1-ab28-4209-a6a8-15ef8e497dd8
records.notes:
Environics Research Group was retained to conduct a survey to evaluate the effectiveness of the Financial Benefits for Canadian Families advertising campaign on behalf of Human Resources and Skills Development Canada (HRSDC). In total, 1,203 telephone interviews were completed from March 7-15, 2011 with a representative sample of Canadians living in the 10 provinces and an oversample of parents of children under the age of 18.

The Government of Canada has maintained a long-standing commitment to help support families with young children by offering and funding a variety of services and financial benefits, including child care benefits, tax benefits and savings plan options.  The Financial Benefits for Canadian Families campaign is an advertising initiative that was designed to inform Canadian parents of the Government of Canada’s programs and financial measures that can help support families with young children. Human Resources and Skills Development Canada was selected to lead a multimedia advertising campaign targeted at Canadian parents.

The campaign included information related to the following Government of Canada programs:

    Universal Child Care Benefit (UCCB)
    Canada Child Tax Benefit (CCTB)
    Children’s Fitness Tax Credit
    Child Care Expense Deduction
    Canada Education Savings Program
    Canada Learning Bond
    Child Disability Benefit
    Registered Disability Savings Plan (RDSP)
    Canada Student Loans and Grants Program

The overall objectives of the advertising campaign were to:

    Raise awareness among Canadian parents of the financial resources available from the Government of Canada to help them raise their children in a nurturing environment;
    Drive Canadians to the hrsdc-rhdcc.gc.ca/families site, and 1 800 O-Canada line to access streamlined, useful information on financial support available to them.

notes_translated.fr:
Environics Research Group a été retenu pour effectuer un sondage visant à évaluer l’efficacité de la campagne publicitaire relative aux prestations financières pour les familles canadiennes au nom de Ressources humaines et Développement des compétences Canada (RHDCC). Au total, 1 203 entrevues téléphoniques ont été effectuées entre le 7 et le 15 mars 2011 auprès d’un échantillon représentatif de Canadiens vivant dans les 10 provinces du pays et d’un suréchantillon de parents d’enfants âgés de moins de 18 ans

Le gouvernement du Canada maintient depuis longtemps son engagement d’aider les familles avec de jeunes enfants en offrant et en finançant une gamme de services et de prestations financières, notamment des prestations pour la garde d’enfants, des économies d’impôts et divers choix de régimes d’épargne. La campagne relative aux prestations financières pour les familles canadiennes est une initiative publicitaire visant à informer les parents canadiens au sujet des mesures financières et des programmes offerts par le gouvernement du Canada afin de soutenir les familles avec de jeunes enfants. RHDCC a été choisi pour mener une campagne publicitaire multimédia à l’intention des parents canadiens.

La campagne comprenait des renseignements en lien avec les programmes du gouvernement du Canada suivants :

    Prestation universelle pour la garde d’enfants
    Prestation fiscale canadienne pour enfants (PFCE)
    Crédit d’impôt pour la condition physique des enfants
    Déduction pour frais de garde d’enfants
    Programme canadien pour l’épargne-études
    Programme canadien pour l’épargne-études
    Prestation pour enfants handicapés
    Régime enregistré d’épargne-invalidité (REEI)
    Programme canadien de prêts aux étudiants et le Programme canadien de bourses aux étudiants

Les objectifs globaux de la campagne publicitaire étaient les suivants :

    Sensibiliser les parents canadiens aux ressources financières mises à leur disposition par le gouvernement du Canada afin de les aider à élever leurs enfants dans un milieu stimulant;
    Inciter les Canadiens à consulter la section sur les familles du site hrsdc-rhdcc.gc.ca et à composer le 1 800 O­Canada afin d’avoir accès à des renseignements simplifiés et utiles au sujet de l’aide financière mise à leur disposition.
notes_translated.en:
Environics Research Group was retained to conduct a survey to evaluate the effectiveness of the Financial Benefits for Canadian Families advertising campaign on behalf of Human Resources and Skills Development Canada (HRSDC). In total, 1,203 telephone interviews were completed from March 7-15, 2011 with a representative sample of Canadians living in the 10 provinces and an oversample of parents of children under the age of 18.

The Government of Canada has maintained a long-standing commitment to help support families with young children by offering and funding a variety of services and financial benefits, including child care benefits, tax benefits and savings plan options.  The Financial Benefits for Canadian Families campaign is an advertising initiative that was designed to inform Canadian parents of the Government of Canada’s programs and financial measures that can help support families with young children. Human Resources and Skills Development Canada was selected to lead a multimedia advertising campaign targeted at Canadian parents.

The campaign included information related to the following Government of Canada programs:

    Universal Child Care Benefit (UCCB)
    Canada Child Tax Benefit (CCTB)
    Children’s Fitness Tax Credit
    Child Care Expense Deduction
    Canada Education Savings Program
    Canada Learning Bond
    Child Disability Benefit
    Registered Disability Savings Plan (RDSP)
    Canada Student Loans and Grants Program

The overall objectives of the advertising campaign were to:

    Raise awareness among Canadian parents of the financial resources available from the Government of Canada to help them raise their children in a nurturing environment;
    Drive Canadians to the hrsdc-rhdcc.gc.ca/families site, and 1 800 O-Canada line to access streamlined, useful information on financial support available to them.

records.name: 5207c3c7-4fff-41f0-aef6-8fefdbde8000
records.notes: The Essential Skills Playbook projects map is developed to highlight projects featured as part of the “Essential Skills Playbook” published by the Office of Literacy and Essential Skills program (OLES) at Employment and Social Development Canada (ESDC). The playbook is developed as a showcase for sharing promising practices, case studies and partnerships, based on OLES projects that were funded through grants and contributions generally dating back to 2012. This map allows users to visualize OLES-funded projects and explore various data variables such as the targeted groups, essential skills, and industry sectors of each project.

notes_translated.fr: La carte qui accompagne le « Guide sur les compétences essentielles » a été élaborée pour mettre en évidence les projets présentés dans le cadre du « Guide sur les compétences essentielles » publié par le Bureau de l'alphabétisation et du programme des compétences essentielles (BACE) d'Emploi et Développement Social Canada (EDSC). Le guide est conçu comme une vitrine pour le partage de pratiques prometteuses, d'études de cas et de partenariats fondés sur des projets du BACE qui ont été financés par des subventions et des contributions remontant généralement à 2012. Cette carte permet aux utilisateurs de visualiser les projets financés par le BACE et d'explorer diverses variables de données comme les groupes ciblés, les compétences essentielles et les projets de chaque secteurs industriels.
notes_translated.en: The Essential Skills Playbook projects map is developed to highlight projects featured as part of the “Essential Skills Playbook” published by the Office of Literacy and Essential Skills program (OLES) at Employment and Social Development Canada (ESDC). The playbook is developed as a showcase for sharing promising practices, case studies and partnerships, based on OLES projects that were funded through grants and contributions generally dating back to 2012. This map allows users to visualize OLES-funded projects and explore various data variables such as the targeted groups, essential skills, and industry sectors of each project.

Le numero de dossier permet d'acceder a l'ensemble de donnees du Systeme de gestion de l'information sur le logement abordable de Subventions Ontario pour obtenir de plus amples renseignements sur le projet, y compris le financement et l'emplacement. *[PILAO]: Programme d’investissement dans le logement abordable

records.name: ca0abf23-2ff1-4505-8769-cc51e3e57bed
records.notes: ADR is a strategy to streamline court processes and encourage alternatives to court. It focuses on a more strengths-based, inclusive and collaborative approach to resolving child protection disputes, and encourages the involvement and support of the family, extended family, and the community, in planning and decision-making for children. The dataset describes the number of referrals in child protection cases that are made to ADR service providers funded by the Ministry of Children and Youth Services, including referrals tracked by Children's Aid Societies. An ADR service provider is a third-party transfer payment agency that is funded by the Ministry of Children and Youth Services to deliver and/or coordinate the delivery of ADR services. The data set is broken down by: * fiscal year * ministry region * children's aid society * total number of new referrals * number of referrals that are carried over from the previous fiscal year * referrals made prior to court involvement * referrals made post court involvement * referrals declined *[ADR]: alternative dispute resolution

notes_translated.en: ADR is a strategy to streamline court processes and encourage alternatives to court. It focuses on a more strengths-based, inclusive and collaborative approach to resolving child protection disputes, and encourages the involvement and support of the family, extended family, and the community, in planning and decision-making for children. The dataset describes the number of referrals in child protection cases that are made to ADR service providers funded by the Ministry of Children and Youth Services, including referrals tracked by Children's Aid Societies. An ADR service provider is a third-party transfer payment agency that is funded by the Ministry of Children and Youth Services to deliver and/or coordinate the delivery of ADR services. The data set is broken down by: * fiscal year * ministry region * children's aid society * total number of new referrals * number of referrals that are carried over from the previous fiscal year * referrals made prior to court involvement * referrals made post court involvement * referrals declined *[ADR]: alternative dispute resolution
notes_translated.fr: Le RED est une strategie permettant de rationaliser les processus judiciaires et de favoriser le reglement extrajudiciaire. Axe sur une methode inclusive et fondee sur la collaboration et les points forts pour resoudre les differends en matiere de protection de l'enfance, il encourage la famille, la famille elargie et la communaute a appuyer et a participer a la planification et a la prise de decisions concernant les enfants. L'ensemble de donnees decrit le nombre d'aiguillages dans les cas de protection de l'enfance qui sont arrives chez des fournisseurs de services RED finances par le ministere des Services a l'enfance et a la jeunesse y compris les aiguillages suivis par les societes d'aide a l'enfance. Un fournisseur de services RED est un organisme tiers pour les paiements de transfert finance par le ministere des Services a l'enfance et a la jeunesse afin de fournir ou de coordonner la prestation des services RED. L'ensemble de donnees est ventile par : * exercice financier * region du Ministere * Societe d'aide a l'enfance * nombre total de nouveaux aiguillages * nombre d'aiguillages qui ont ete reportes de l'exercice financier precedent * nombre d'aiguillages effectues avant le recours au tribunal * nombre d'aiguillages effectues apres le recours au tribunal * aiguillages refuses *[RED]: règlement extrajudiciaire des différends

Climate finance is offered by a wide variety of actors in the form of grants, debt financing, risk mitigation, equity and carbon offsets. Climate finance is available to businesses that deliver goods, works or services for projects related to climate change mitigation, adaptation and REDD+ (reduce emissions from deforestation and forest degradation). These fact sheets provide information on how to access climate-specific business opportunities for seven multilateral development banks and funds"--Provided by publisher."

« Différents intervenants offrent du financement pour la lutte contre les changements climatiques sous la forme de subventions, de financement par emprunt, d’atténuation des risques, de capitaux propres et de compensation des émissions de carbone. Le financement pour la lutte contre les changements climatiques est offert aux entreprises qui fournissent des biens, des travaux ou des services liés aux projets d’atténuation des changements climatiques, d’adaptation aux changements climatiques ou de REDD+ (réduction des émissions de la déforestation et de la dégradation des forêts). Ces fiches de renseignements fournissent de l’information sur la façon d’accéder à des débouchés propres à la lutte contre les changements climatiques pour sept des fonds et des banques multilatérales de développement » -- Fourni par l'éditeur.
